questions.question: Who appoints the Chief Minister of a State?
questions.explanation: The Governor appoints the Chief Minister, who must enjoy the confidence of the Legislative Assembly.

questions.question: A State Legislative Assembly (Vidhan Sabha) member must be at least:
questions.explanation: To be a member of a State Legislative Assembly (Vidhan Sabha), a person must be at least 25 years old (Article 173). To vote, the minimum age is 18 years (reduced from 21 by the 61st Amendment, 1988).

questions.question: Tamil Nadu's Legislative Assembly is called:
options: Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly (it has unicameral legislature — only one house)
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu has a unicameral legislature — only a Legislative Assembly (not a Vidhan Parishad/Legislative Council). It has 234 constituencies (234 MLAs). Tamil Nadu abolished the Legislative Council in 1986.

questions.question: The Advocate General of a State is appointed by:
questions.explanation: Article 165: Advocate General is appointed by the Governor. The Advocate General is the chief law officer of the state, analogous to the Attorney General of India at the Union level.

questions.question: The status of Delhi as National Capital Territory was given by:
questions.explanation: 69th Constitutional Amendment (1991) gave Delhi the status of National Capital Territory (NCT) with its own Legislative Assembly of 70 seats.

questions.question: Chief Minister is appointed by:
questions.explanation: Chief Minister is appointed by the Governor. Conventionally, the leader of the majority party/coalition in the State Legislative Assembly is appointed CM. CM need not be a sitting MLA.

questions.question: The Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly has how many seats?
questions.explanation: The Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly (Vidhan Sabha) has 234 seats (232 general + 2 Anglo-Indian nominated seats, though the 104th Amendment abolished Anglo-Indian nomination from 2020). Currently 234 elected seats.

questions.question: When was the State of Tamil Nadu officially renamed from Madras State?
questions.explanation: Madras State was renamed Tamil Nadu on 14 January 1969 under Chief Minister C.N. Annadurai. 'Tamil Nadu' means 'Land of Tamils' in Tamil.

questions.question: The Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly is:
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu has a unicameral legislature — only the Legislative Assembly (Vidhan Sabha). It had a Legislative Council (Vidhan Parishad) but it was abolished in 1986.

questions.question: The first Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu was:
questions.explanation: O.P. Ramaswamy Reddiyar was the first Chief Minister of Madras State (1947-1949) after independence. Chakravarti Rajagopalachari became the first CM under the new Constitution in 1952.

questions.question: Jayalalithaa served as Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu how many times?
questions.explanation: J. Jayalalithaa served as Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu 6 times: 1991-96, 2001, 2002-06, 2011-14, 2015, 2016-17. She was the leader of AIADMK from 1989 till her death in December 2016.

questions.question: The maximum strength of a State Legislative Assembly is:
questions.explanation: Article 170: State Legislative Assembly (Vidhan Sabha) can have not more than 500 members and not less than 60 members (except Goa, Sikkim, Mizoram, Nagaland have less than 60).

questions.question: Tamil Nadu State Legislature is:
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu has a unicameral legislature — only the Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly (Vidhan Sabha) with 234 members. The Tamil Nadu Legislative Council was abolished in 1986.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution provides for the establishment of Panchayats?
questions.explanation: Article 243 of the Constitution provides for the establishment of Panchayats in India.

questions.question: The Governor of a State is appointed by whom?
questions.explanation: The Governor of a State is appointed by the President of India under Article 155.

questions.question: Which part of the Indian Constitution deals with the Directive Principles of State Policy?
questions.explanation: Part IV of the Constitution (Articles 36-51) contains the Directive Principles of State Policy.

questions.question: The minimum age for becoming a member of the Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly is:
questions.explanation: A person must be at least 25 years old to be a member of the State Legislative Assembly.

questions.question: Which of the following is a Fundamental Right under the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Right to Education (Article 21A) is a Fundamental Right; others are not.

questions.question: The term 'Secular' in the Preamble of the Indian Constitution means:
questions.explanation: Secular means the State treats all religions equally and has no official religion.

questions.question: Who is the ex-officio Chairman of the Tamil Nadu State Planning Commission?
questions.explanation: The Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu is the ex-officio Chairman of the State Planning Commission.

questions.question: Under the Panchayati Raj system, the Gram Sabha consists of:
options: All adult residents of the village
questions.explanation: Gram Sabha comprises all adult persons registered as voters in the village.

questions.question: The power to promulgate an Ordinance in a State rests with the:
questions.explanation: The Governor can promulgate Ordinances under Article 213 when the legislature is not in session.

questions.question: Which Tamil Nadu government scheme provides free breakfast to students in government schools?
questions.explanation: The Chief Minister's Breakfast Scheme provides free breakfast to students in government primary schools.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution defines the Gram Sabha?
questions.explanation: Article 243A defines the Gram Sabha as a body consisting of persons registered in the electoral rolls of a village.

questions.question: The Directive Principles of State Policy are contained in which Part of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Part IV (Articles 36-51) contains the Directive Principles of State Policy.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution prohibits the practice of untouchability?
questions.explanation: Article 17 abolishes 'untouchability' and forbids its practice in any form.

questions.question: Who appoints the Governor of a state in India?
questions.explanation: The Governor of a state is appointed by the President of India under Article 155.

questions.question: What is the term of office for members of Panchayats (village-level local bodies)?
questions.explanation: Article 243E provides that every Panchayat shall continue for five years from its first meeting.

questions.question: Which word was added to the Preamble of the Indian Constitution by the 42nd Amendment?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) added the words 'Socialist', 'Secular', and 'Integrity' to the Preamble.

questions.question: Judges of a High Court are appointed by whom?
questions.explanation: Under Article 217, the President appoints High Court judges after consultation with the Chief Justice of India and the Governor.

questions.question: The State Election Commission is responsible for conducting elections to which bodies?
questions.explanation: The State Election Commission conducts elections to Panchayats and Municipalities as per Article 243K and 243ZA.

questions.question: Which article provides for reservation of seats for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes in Panchayats?
questions.explanation: Article 243D provides for reservation of seats for SCs/STs in every Panchayat.

questions.question: Fundamental Duties were added to the Indian Constitution by which Amendment?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) inserted Part IVA containing Fundamental Duties under Article 51A.

questions.question: Under which part of the Indian Constitution are the provisions related to the State Executive and Legislature found?
questions.explanation: Part VI (Articles 152-237) of the Constitution deals with the State Government, including the Executive and Legislature.

questions.question: Who appoints the Governor of a state?
questions.explanation: The Governor is appointed by the President of India under Article 155 of the Constitution.

questions.question: What is the minimum age required to become a member of the State Legislative Assembly?
questions.explanation: Article 173 prescribes the minimum age of 25 years for membership in the State Legislative Assembly.

questions.question: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act is related to which of the following?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment (1992) constitutionalized Panchayati Raj institutions in India.

questions.question: The term of office of a member of the State Legislative Council (where it exists) is?
questions.explanation: Members of the Legislative Council have a term of 6 years, with one-third retiring every two years.

questions.question: Which schedule of the Constitution contains provisions for the administration of Scheduled Areas and Scheduled Tribes in states?
questions.explanation: The Fifth Schedule deals with the administration of Scheduled Areas and Scheduled Tribes in states (except northeastern states).

questions.question: The concept of 'District Planning Committee' is mentioned in which article of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Article 243ZD provides for the constitution of District Planning Committees to consolidate plans from Panchayats and Municipalities.

questions.question: The 'Amma Unavagam' scheme, providing subsidized meals, is a flagship initiative of which state government?
questions.explanation: Amma Unavagam (canteens) were launched by the Tamil Nadu government to provide affordable meals to the public.

questions.question: The power to decide disqualification of a member of the State Legislature under the anti-defection law lies with?
questions.explanation: Under the Tenth Schedule, the Speaker (or Chairman) of the House decides on disqualification for defection.

questions.question: Which article of the Constitution provides for the establishment of a State Finance Commission?
questions.explanation: Article 243I mandates the formation of a State Finance Commission for Panchayats; Article 243Y does the same for Municipalities.

questions.question: According to Article 164, who appoints the Chief Minister of a state?
questions.explanation: The Governor appoints the Chief Minister under Article 164.

questions.question: What is the maximum strength of a state's Legislative Council as per Article 171?
questions.explanation: Article 171 states that the total number of members in the Legislative Council shall not exceed one-third of the total number of members in the Legislative Assembly.

questions.question: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act is related to which local self-government?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment Act, 1992, provides a constitutional framework for Panchayati Raj institutions.

questions.question: Who conducts elections to Panchayats in a state?
questions.explanation: Under Article 243K, the State Election Commission is responsible for conducting Panchayat elections.

questions.question: In which year was the state of Madras officially renamed as Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: Madras State was renamed Tamil Nadu on January 14, 1969, under the Madras State (Alteration of Name) Act.

questions.question: Under Article 200, the Governor may reserve a bill passed by the state legislature for the consideration of whom?
questions.explanation: Article 200 allows the Governor to reserve a bill for the President's consideration.

questions.question: The Governor holds office during the pleasure of whom?
questions.explanation: Under Article 156, the Governor serves at the pleasure of the President.

questions.question: Which of the following subjects is included in the State List (List II) of the Seventh Schedule?
questions.explanation: Police is a state subject under Entry 1 of the State List, while Defence, Currency, and Railways are Union subjects.

questions.question: The Governor can promulgate ordinances when the State Legislature is not in session under which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 213 empowers the Governor to promulgate ordinances during recess of the State Legislature.

questions.question: Which part of the Indian Constitution contains the Directive Principles of State Policy?
questions.explanation: Part IV (Articles 36-51) of the Indian Constitution contains the Directive Principles of State Policy.

questions.question: The immunity of the Governor of a state from criminal proceedings extends to which period?
options: During the pleasure of the President
During the term of the Assembly
questions.explanation: Under Article 361, the Governor enjoys immunity from criminal proceedings during his entire term of office.

questions.question: Under the Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj Act, the term of office of a Grama Panchayat President is?
questions.explanation: The Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj Act prescribes a 5-year term for Grama Panchayat Presidents, as per Article 243E.

questions.question: Which schedule of the Indian Constitution distributes legislative powers between the Union and the States?
questions.explanation: The Seventh Schedule contains three lists (Union, State, Concurrent) distributing legislative subjects between the Union and States.

questions.question: The 'Right to Constitutional Remedies' under Article 32 of the Indian Constitution is a?
questions.explanation: Article 32 enshrines the Right to Constitutional Remedies as a Fundamental Right, allowing citizens to approach the Supreme Court.

questions.question: In Tamil Nadu, the State Election Commission is responsible for conducting elections to which bodies?
questions.explanation: Under Article 243K, the State Election Commission conducts elections to all local self-government bodies, including panchayats and municipalities.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a source of law in the Indian judicial system?
questions.explanation: Executive orders are not a source of law; legislation, precedent, and custom are recognized sources in the Indian legal system.

questions.question: The Preamble of the Indian Constitution declares India to be a?
questions.explanation: The Preamble describes India as a 'Union of States', indicating its indestructible nature unlike a federation.

questions.question: Under the Tamil Nadu government’s 'Amma Unavagam' scheme, what is the primary objective?
questions.explanation: The Amma Unavagam scheme provides highly subsidized nutritious meals to the urban and rural poor in Tamil Nadu.

questions.question: The concept of 'Judicial Review' in India is derived from which country's constitution?
questions.explanation: India borrowed the concept of Judicial Review from the USA, where the Supreme Court can declare laws unconstitutional.

questions.question: What is the minimum reservation for women in local bodies under the Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj Act?
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu provides a statutory reservation of 33% (one-third) of seats for women in local bodies.

questions.question: Which Schedule of the Indian Constitution contains the powers, authority, and responsibilities of Panchayats?
questions.explanation: The Eleventh Schedule (Article 243G) lists 29 subjects for Panchayats under the 73rd Amendment.

questions.question: Under the Indian Constitution, the Governor of a state is appointed by which authority?
options: Chief Justice of the High Court
questions.explanation: Article 155 of the Constitution vests the appointment of the Governor with the President of India.

questions.question: Which amendment to the Indian Constitution led to the establishment of the three-tier Panchayati Raj system in Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment (1992) added Part IX to the Constitution, mandating the three-tier Panchayati Raj system.

questions.question: Which article of the Constitution provides for the establishment of a State Election Commission for Panchayati Raj institutions?
questions.explanation: Article 243K mandates the creation of a State Election Commission for superintendence of Panchayat elections.

questions.question: In Tamil Nadu, what is the minimum age required to contest an election to the State Legislative Assembly?
questions.explanation: As per Article 173 of the Constitution, the minimum age for membership in a state legislative assembly is 25 years.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a power of the Governor of a state?
options: Appointment of judges of the High Court
questions.explanation: Judges of a High Court are appointed by the President after consultation with the Chief Justice and Governor.

questions.question: The 74th Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992 deals with which form of local self-government?
questions.explanation: The 74th Amendment added Part IX-A to the Constitution, covering urban local bodies or Municipalities.

questions.question: What is the tenure of a Governor of a state under normal circumstances?
options: During the pleasure of the President
questions.explanation: Article 156 states that the Governor holds office during the pleasure of the President, but the usual term is 5 years.

questions.question: Which schedule of the Indian Constitution contains the State List of subjects?
questions.explanation: The Seventh Schedule lists the Union List, State List, and Concurrent List under Article 246.

questions.question: The power to disqualify a member of the Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly on grounds of defection rests with whom?
questions.explanation: Under the Tenth Schedule (anti-defection law), the Speaker decides on disqualification of assembly members.

questions.question: In the three-tier Panchayati Raj system of Tamil Nadu, which is the highest tier?
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu's Panchayati Raj comprises Village Panchayat (lowest), Panchayat Union (block), and District Panchayat (top).

questions.question: The Chief Minister of a state is appointed by the
questions.explanation: Under Article 164, the Chief Minister is appointed by the Governor.

questions.question: Which of the following states has a bicameral legislature?
questions.explanation: Maharashtra has a Legislative Council (Vidhan Parishad) in addition to the Legislative Assembly.

questions.question: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act is associated with
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment (1992) added Part IX on Panchayats.

questions.question: Elections to Panchayats are conducted by the
questions.explanation: Article 243K provides for a State Election Commission to conduct panchayat elections.

questions.question: Which Directive Principle of State Policy directs the State to organise village panchayats?
questions.explanation: Article 40 in Part IV of the Constitution directs the State to organise village panchayats.

questions.question: The Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj Act was enacted in the year
questions.explanation: The Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj Act, 1994 governs local self-government in rural areas.

questions.question: The State Finance Commission is constituted under which Article of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Article 243I provides for the constitution of a State Finance Commission every five years.

questions.question: The Governor of a state can promulgate an ordinance under which Article when the legislature is not in session?
questions.explanation: Article 213 gives the Governor power to promulgate ordinances during recess of the state legislature.

questions.question: The Chief Minister of a state holds office during the pleasure of the
questions.explanation: Under Article 164, the Chief Minister holds office during the pleasure of the Governor.

questions.question: Under the Indian Constitution, the executive power of a State is vested in whom?
questions.explanation: Article 154 of the Indian Constitution vests the executive power of the State in the Governor, who exercises it directly or through subordinate officers.

questions.question: The Panchayati Raj system in India was first introduced in which state?
questions.explanation: Panchayati Raj was first introduced in Rajasthan (Nagaur district) on October 2, 1959, followed soon after by Andhra Pradesh.

questions.question: A person convicted of a criminal offence and sentenced to imprisonment for at least two years is disqualified from voting for how many years after release?
questions.explanation: Under the Representation of the People Act, 1951, such a person is disqualified for six years from the date of release.

questions.question: Which Schedule of the Indian Constitution contains provisions regarding the administration and control of Scheduled Areas and Scheduled Tribes in a state?
questions.explanation: The Fifth Schedule deals with the administration and control of Scheduled Areas and Scheduled Tribes in any state except the four northeastern states covered by the Sixth Schedule.

questions.question: In the state judiciary, who appoints the judges of a High Court?
options: Chief Justice of the High Court
questions.explanation: Judges of a High Court are appointed by the President of India in consultation with the Chief Justice of India, the Governor of the state, and the Chief Justice of the High Court.

questions.question: Under the Tamil Nadu Panchayats Act, what is the minimum age qualification for a person to be elected as a member of a Gram Panchayat?
questions.explanation: For elections to local bodies in Tamil Nadu, the minimum age is 21 years, unlike 18 years for State Legislative Assembly elections.

questions.question: The Directive Principles of State Policy in the Indian Constitution are borrowed from the Constitution of which country?
questions.explanation: The Directive Principles were borrowed from the Constitution of Ireland, which had itself borrowed them from the Spanish Constitution.

questions.question: The power of the Governor to promulgate an ordinance when the State Legislature is not in session is derived from which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 213 empowers the Governor to promulgate ordinances during the recess of the State Legislature, subject to limitations similar to those on the President under Article 123.

questions.question: Which of the following is a mandatory (compulsory) function of a District Planning Committee (DPC) in Tamil Nadu as per the 74th Constitutional Amendment?
questions.explanation: The DPC is constitutionally mandated to consolidate the plans prepared by the panchayats and municipalities in the district into a draft district development plan.

questions.question: The 'Doctrine of Basic Structure' of the Constitution was propounded by the Supreme Court in which landmark case?
questions.explanation: The Supreme Court in the Kesavananda Bharati case (1973) held that Parliament cannot amend the basic structure of the Constitution, including its federal features and judicial review.

questions.question: Which amendment to the Indian Constitution added the words 'Socialist' and 'Secular' to the Preamble?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) inserted 'Socialist', 'Secular', and 'Integrity' into the Preamble.

questions.question: Under which Article of the Indian Constitution can a person file a writ of Habeas Corpus for unlawful detention?
questions.explanation: Article 32 (Right to Constitutional Remedies) empowers the Supreme Court to issue writs including Habeas Corpus.

questions.question: The Directive Principle under Article 40 of the Indian Constitution directs the State to organize which type of local self-government?
questions.explanation: Article 40 (in Part IV) requires the State to take steps to organize Village Panchayats as units of self-government.

questions.question: Which Schedule of the Indian Constitution lists the subjects under the purview of Panchayati Raj institutions?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment added the Eleventh Schedule containing 29 functional items for Panchayats.

questions.question: The Chief Electoral Officer of Tamil Nadu is appointed by which authority?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission of India appoints the Chief Electoral Officer of each state under Article 324.

questions.question: Under Article 226 of the Indian Constitution, a High Court has the power to issue writs for the enforcement of which rights?
options: Fundamental Rights and other legal rights
questions.explanation: Article 226 empowers High Courts to issue writs for enforcement of Fundamental Rights and for any other purpose.

questions.question: In which year was the Tamil Nadu Legislative Council abolished?
questions.explanation: The Tamil Nadu Legislative Council was abolished by the Tamil Nadu Legislative Council (Abolition) Act, 1986.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution provides for the appointment of the Governor of a state?
questions.explanation: Article 155 states that the Governor of a state shall be appointed by the President by warrant under his hand and seal.

questions.question: Which Schedule of the Indian Constitution contains the subjects for Urban Local Bodies under the 74th Amendment?
questions.explanation: The 74th Amendment added the Twelfth Schedule listing 18 functions for Municipalities.

questions.question: Which of the following is a flagship social welfare scheme launched by the Tamil Nadu state government?
questions.explanation: Kalaignar Magalir Urimai Thittam is a Tamil Nadu government scheme providing monthly financial assistance to women heads of families.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution deals with the appointment of the Governor of a state?
questions.explanation: Article 155 states that the Governor shall be appointed by the President by warrant under his hand and seal.

questions.question: The Panchayati Raj system in India was constitutionalized through which amendment?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act of 1992 gave constitutional status to Panchayati Raj institutions.

questions.question: Which schedule of the Indian Constitution contains provisions regarding the administration of scheduled and tribal areas in states?
questions.explanation: The Fifth Schedule deals with administration and control of Scheduled Areas and Scheduled Tribes in states except Assam, Meghalaya, Tripura, and Mizoram.

questions.question: Under which article of the Indian Constitution can the Governor reserve a bill for the consideration of the President?
questions.explanation: Article 200 gives the Governor the power to reserve a bill passed by the state legislature for the President's consideration.

questions.question: Which committee's recommendations led to the establishment of Panchayati Raj institutions in India?
questions.explanation: The Balwant Rai Mehta Committee (1957) recommended the establishment of a three-tier Panchayati Raj system.

questions.question: The Governor of a state is appointed by the President and holds office during the pleasure of the President. This is mentioned in which article?
questions.explanation: Article 156 states that the Governor shall hold office during the pleasure of the President.

questions.question: Which among the following is a part of the State List under the Seventh Schedule of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Police is a state subject under Entry 2 of the State List; Education is in Concurrent List, Banking and Defence in Union List.

questions.question: The concept of 'Directive Principles of State Policy' in the Indian Constitution was borrowed from which country's constitution?
questions.explanation: The Directive Principles were inspired by the Irish Constitution.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution provides for reservation of seats for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes in the Legislative Assembly of every state?
questions.explanation: Article 332 provides for reservation of seats for SCs and STs in state legislative assemblies.

questions.question: The Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj Act was enacted in which year?
questions.explanation: The Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj Act, 1994 was enacted to implement the 73rd Amendment in the state.

questions.question: Under which Article of the Constitution of India can the Governor of a state reserve a bill for the consideration of the President?
questions.explanation: Article 200 empowers the Governor to reserve certain bills for the President's consideration.

questions.question: Which principle of the Directive Principles of State Policy is specifically related to the formation of Panchayats in India?
questions.explanation: Article 40 directs the State to organize village panchayats as units of self-government.

questions.question: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992 added which Part to the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Part IX, dealing with Panchayats, was inserted by the 73rd Amendment.

questions.question: In the context of Tamil Nadu, which authority conducts elections to the local bodies?
questions.explanation: The State Election Commission is responsible for conducting local body elections in Tamil Nadu.

questions.question: Which Fundamental Right was added to the Constitution through the 86th Amendment Act, 2002?
questions.explanation: Article 21A, ensuring free and compulsory education for children aged 6-14, was inserted by the 86th Amendment.

questions.question: The term 'State' for the purpose of Part III (Fundamental Rights) is defined in which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 12 defines 'State' for the enforcement of Fundamental Rights.

questions.question: Which Article empowers the High Court to issue writs for enforcement of fundamental rights?
questions.explanation: Article 226 gives High Courts the power to issue writs for fundamental rights and other purposes.

questions.question: The 'Eleventh Schedule' of the Indian Constitution specifies subjects under the jurisdiction of which institution?
questions.explanation: The Eleventh Schedule lists 29 functional items for Panchayati Raj institutions.

questions.question: As per the Constitution of India, the Governor of a state is appointed by the
questions.explanation: Article 155 states that the Governor is appointed by the President of India.

questions.question: Which Tamil Nadu government scheme aims to provide nutritious breakfast to primary school students?
questions.explanation: The Chief Minister's Breakfast Scheme was launched in Tamil Nadu in 2022-23 for primary school children.

questions.question: Under which Article can the President remove a Governor from office?
questions.explanation: Article 156 states that the Governor holds office during the pleasure of the President, implying removal by the President.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a Fundamental Right under Part III of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Right to Property (Article 300A) is a constitutional right but not a Fundamental Right, removed by the 44th Amendment.

questions.question: The Tamil Nadu Panchayats Act, which governs the rural local self-government, was enacted in which year?
questions.explanation: The Tamil Nadu Panchayats Act was enacted in 1994, following the 73rd Constitutional Amendment.

questions.question: The Directive Principles of State Policy are enshrined in which Part of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Part IV (Articles 36-51) contains the Directive Principles of State Policy.

questions.question: Which Article of the Constitution vests the executive power of the state in the Governor, yet the real authority is exercised by the Chief Minister and Council of Ministers?
questions.explanation: Article 163 states that there shall be a Council of Ministers to aid and advise the Governor in the exercise of his functions.

questions.question: What is the current number of seats in the Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly?
questions.explanation: The Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly has 234 elected members, as per the Delimitation Commission.

questions.question: The word 'Secular' was added to the Preamble of the Indian Constitution by which Amendment?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) added 'Socialist', 'Secular' and 'Integrity' to the Preamble.

questions.question: In the Panchayati Raj system, the Gram Sabha consists of which of the following?
options: Elected members of the Gram Panchayat
All adult citizens of the village
All persons registered in the electoral roll of the village
questions.explanation: Gram Sabha comprises all persons whose names are included in the electoral roll for the Panchayat area.

questions.question: Which of the following Indian states currently has a unicameral legislature?
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu abolished its Legislative Council in 1986, making it a unicameral state.

questions.question: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act added which part to the Constitution of India?
questions.explanation: Part IX deals with Panchayats and was added by the 73rd Amendment in 1992.

questions.question: Under the Directive Principles, which Article directs the state to promote the educational and economic interests of SC/ST and other weaker sections?
questions.explanation: Article 46 mandates the state to promote the educational and economic interests of SC/ST and other weaker sections.

questions.question: The 'Tamil Nadu Protection of Interests of Depositors (in Financial Establishments) Act' is primarily aimed at:
questions.explanation: This Tamil Nadu Act is designed to protect depositors from fraudulent financial establishments.

questions.question: Under Article 200 of the Constitution, what options does a Governor have with a bill passed by the state legislature?
questions.explanation: Article 200 allows the Governor to assent, withhold assent, return for reconsideration, or reserve the bill for the President.

questions.question: Who appoints the judges of a High Court in India?
options: Chief Justice of the High Court
questions.explanation: Judges of High Courts are appointed by the President after consultation with the CJI, Governor, and Chief Justice of that High Court.

questions.question: Which Fundamental Right prohibits the employment of children below the age of 14 in hazardous occupations?
questions.explanation: Article 24 prohibits child labour in factories, mines, or other hazardous jobs for children under 14.

questions.question: Who is responsible for conducting elections to Panchayati Raj institutions in a state?
questions.explanation: The State Election Commission conducts elections to local bodies as per the 73rd and 74th Amendments.

questions.question: Which words were added to the Preamble of the Indian Constitution by the 42nd Amendment?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) added 'Socialist', 'Secular', and 'Integrity' to the Preamble.

questions.question: In which year was the Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj Act enacted?
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu enacted its Panchayati Raj Act in 1994, following the 73rd Constitutional Amendment.

questions.question: Which part of the Indian Constitution deals with the state government (State Executive and Legislature)?
questions.explanation: Part VI (Articles 152-237) deals with the state executive and legislature.

questions.question: Under the Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj system, what is the apex body at the district level?
questions.explanation: The District Panchayat is the highest tier in the three-tier Panchayati Raj system in Tamil Nadu.

questions.question: The State Legislature in Tamil Nadu consists of ______?
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu has a unicameral legislature; the Legislative Council was abolished in 1986.

questions.question: The Tamil Nadu State Election Commission is responsible for conducting elections to which of the following?
questions.explanation: The State Election Commission conducts elections to local bodies (Panchayats and Municipalities).

questions.question: Which schedule of the Constitution contains provisions regarding the administration of scheduled areas and tribes?
questions.explanation: The Fifth Schedule relates to administration of Scheduled Areas and Scheduled Tribes in states other than Assam, Meghalaya, etc.

questions.question: Under the Directive Principles of State Policy, which article promotes the establishment of village panchayats?
questions.explanation: Article 40 directs the state to organize village panchayats as units of self-government.

questions.question: Which of the following is a state government policy specific to Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu introduced the Free Breakfast Scheme for students in government schools as a state-specific policy.

questions.question: The age of retirement of a Judge of the Tamil Nadu High Court is?
questions.explanation: High Court judges retire at 62 years as per Article 217 of the Constitution.

questions.question: Which amendment to the Indian Constitution introduced the Panchayati Raj system?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment (1992) added Part IX on Panchayats to the Constitution.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution defines the term 'Panchayat'?
questions.explanation: Article 243 provides the definition of Panchayat; Article 243A deals with Gram Sabha.

questions.question: Which Fundamental Right is available only to Indian citizens?
options: Right to freedom of speech and expression
Right to life and personal liberty
questions.explanation: Article 19 guarantees freedom of speech only to citizens; other rights apply to all persons.

questions.question: The Directive Principles of State Policy in the Indian Constitution are borrowed from which country?
questions.explanation: The Directive Principles were inspired by the Irish Constitution.

questions.question: Which type of legislature does the state of Tamil Nadu have?
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu has a Legislative Assembly only, making it unicameral.

questions.question: In Tamil Nadu, the intermediate level of Panchayati Raj is called:
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu's three-tier system includes Gram Panchayat, Panchayat Union, and District Panchayat.

questions.question: Under the 73rd Constitutional Amendment, what is the minimum age to contest Panchayat elections?
questions.explanation: Article 243F sets the minimum age at 21 years for Panchayat elections.

questions.question: The concept of 'Welfare State' in the Indian Constitution is reflected in which part?
questions.explanation: The Directive Principles aim to establish a welfare state.

questions.question: Under which article can the Governor of Tamil Nadu reserve a bill for the President's consideration?
questions.explanation: Article 200 empowers the Governor to reserve a bill for the President's consideration.

questions.question: Under which Article can the Governor address the Legislative Assembly of a state?
questions.explanation: Article 175 empowers the Governor to address the Legislative Assembly or either House of the state legislature.

questions.question: Which Schedule of the Constitution allocates seats to states in the Rajya Sabha?
questions.explanation: The Fourth Schedule (Article 80) deals with the allocation of seats in the Rajya Sabha to states and union territories.

questions.question: The Panchayati Raj system in India is based on which principle?
questions.explanation: Panchayati Raj is based on the principle of decentralization of power to local self-governments.

questions.question: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act is related to which subject?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment (1992) added Part IX to the Constitution, strengthening Panchayati Raj institutions.

questions.question: The 'Basic Structure' doctrine of the Indian Constitution was propounded in which landmark case?
options: Kesavananda Bharati v. State of Kerala
Minerva Mills v. Union of India
S.R. Bommai v. Union of India
questions.explanation: The Kesavananda Bharati case (1973) established that the basic structure of the Constitution cannot be amended.

questions.question: Under which Article can a state create or abolish its Legislative Council?
questions.explanation: Article 169 provides for the creation or abolition of the Legislative Council in a state by Parliament.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a Fundamental Duty under Article 51A?
questions.explanation: Voting is a right, not a duty. Article 51A lists eleven fundamental duties; voting is not among them.

questions.question: The term 'Socialist' in the Preamble of the Indian Constitution signifies?
options: Welfare state only for the poor
questions.explanation: The term 'Socialist' in the Preamble implies social and economic equality, not complete state ownership.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a feature of the State Government in India as per the Constitution?
options: Council of Ministers headed by Chief Minister
questions.explanation: Only some states have a bicameral legislature; most have a unicameral system.

questions.question: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act is associated with which level of local self-government?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment (1992) added Part IX to the Constitution, dealing with Panchayats.

questions.question: In Tamil Nadu, the system of local body elections is conducted by which authority?
questions.explanation: The State Election Commission is responsible for conducting elections to local bodies in Tamil Nadu.

questions.question: Which Directive Principle is specifically aimed at promoting cottage industries in villages?
questions.explanation: Article 43 directs the state to promote cottage industries on an individual or cooperative basis in rural areas.

questions.question: The Governor of a state is appointed by the:
options: Chief Justice of the High Court
questions.explanation: The Governor is the constitutional head of a state and is appointed by the President of India.

questions.question: Which of the following is a discretionary power of the Governor of a state?
options: Summoning and proroguing the state legislature
Appointing members of the State Public Service Commission
questions.explanation: When no party has a clear majority, the Governor uses discretion in appointing the Chief Minister.

questions.question: The concept of 'District Planning Committee' is mentioned in which part of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Part IX (Article 243ZD) provides for the constitution of District Planning Committees in states.

questions.question: Which of the following rights is available only to citizens of India and not to foreigners?
options: Right to freedom of speech and expression
Right to protection of life and personal liberty
questions.explanation: Article 19 (freedom of speech, etc.) is specifically guaranteed only to citizens of India.

questions.question: In the context of Tamil Nadu, the 'Tamil Nadu State Finance Commission' is constituted under which Article of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Article 243-I mandates the creation of a State Finance Commission for Panchayats every five years.

questions.question: What is the maximum strength of the State Legislative Council (Vidhan Parishad) as per the Constitution of India?
options: One-half of the total membership of the Legislative Assembly
One-third of the total membership of the Legislative Assembly
One-fourth of the total membership of the Legislative Assembly
Equal to the total membership of the Legislative Assembly
questions.explanation: Article 171 provides that the total number of members in the Legislative Council shall not exceed one-third of the total membership of the Legislative Assembly.

questions.question: Under which Article of the Constitution can the Governor promulgate ordinances when the State Legislature is not in session?
questions.explanation: Article 213 empowers the Governor to promulgate ordinances during the recess of the State Legislature.

questions.question: The three-tier structure of Panchayati Raj in Tamil Nadu is based on which constitutional amendment?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment (1992) added Part IX to the Constitution, establishing a three-tier Panchayati Raj system.

questions.question: Which constitutional body is responsible for the conduct of elections to Panchayats and Municipalities in a State?
questions.explanation: Article 243K provides for a State Election Commission to supervise local body elections.

questions.question: The judges of a High Court are appointed by the President of India in consultation with which of the following?
options: Chief Justice of India and Governor of the State
Chief Justice of the High Court and Chief Minister
Leader of Opposition in the State Assembly
questions.explanation: As per Article 217, the President appoints High Court judges in consultation with the Chief Justice of India, the Governor, and the Chief Justice of the High Court.

questions.question: Which Directive Principle of State Policy aims to secure the equitable distribution of material resources for the common good?
questions.explanation: Article 39(b) directs the State to ensure that the ownership and control of material resources are distributed to subserve the common good.

questions.question: The right to property in India is currently a:
options: Legal right under the Transfer of Property Act
questions.explanation: The 44th Amendment (1978) removed the right to property from Fundamental Rights and made it a constitutional right under Article 300A.

questions.question: Which term in the Preamble of the Indian Constitution ensures that all citizens have the right to participate in the governance of the country?
questions.explanation: The term 'Democratic' in the Preamble indicates that the government derives its authority from the will of the people through free and fair elections.

questions.question: Under the 73rd Amendment of the Indian Constitution, what is the minimum reservation for women in Panchayati Raj institutions?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment mandates that not less than one-third (33%) of the total seats in Panchayati Raj institutions be reserved for women.

questions.question: The Tamil Nadu Panchayats Act, 1994 provides for a three-tier Panchayati Raj system. Which of the following is NOT a tier?
questions.explanation: The three tiers are Village Panchayat, Panchayat Union Council (block level), and District Panchayat. 'Block Panchayat' is not an official tier.

questions.question: The term 'Secular' was added to the Preamble of the Indian Constitution by which Amendment?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) inserted the words 'Socialist', 'Secular', and 'Integrity' into the Preamble.

questions.question: Which of the following Fundamental Rights is available only to citizens of India and not to foreigners?
questions.explanation: Article 19 (freedom of speech, assembly, etc.) is specifically guaranteed only to citizens, whereas Articles 14, 21, and 32 are available to all persons.

questions.question: The Directive Principle under Article 39(b) of the Indian Constitution relates to:
options: Distribution of ownership of material resources
questions.explanation: Article 39(b) directs the State to ensure that the ownership and control of material resources are distributed to best serve the common good.

questions.question: In Tamil Nadu, the State Election Commission is responsible for conducting elections to which of the following bodies?
questions.explanation: The State Election Commission conducts elections to local self-government bodies such as panchayats and municipalities, not to Parliament or State Legislature.

questions.question: The tenure of a member of the Tamil Nadu Legislative Council (if it exists) is normally:
questions.explanation: Under Article 171, members of a State Legislative Council have a six-year term, with one-third retiring every two years. (Tamil Nadu abolished its Council in 1986.)

questions.question: Which High Court has the largest number of judges in India?
questions.explanation: Allahabad High Court has the highest sanctioned strength of judges (160), making it the largest High Court in India.

questions.question: The 73rd Amendment to the Constitution added which Part to the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment (1992) added Part IX (Articles 243 to 243-O) dealing with Panchayats. Part IX-A was added for Municipalities.

questions.question: Which constitutional amendment added Part IX dealing with Panchayats?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment Act, 1992 added Part IX (Articles 243 to 243O) to the Constitution establishing the Panchayati Raj system.

questions.question: Under which article is the State Election Commission constituted for Panchayat elections?
questions.explanation: Article 243K provides for the constitution of a State Election Commission to supervise, direct, and control elections to Panchayats.

questions.question: Which act governs the Panchayati Raj system in Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: The Tamil Nadu Panchayats Act, 1994 is the primary legislation for rural local bodies in Tamil Nadu.

questions.question: The Governor’s power to grant pardon, reprieve, or remission of punishment for state offences is under which article?
questions.explanation: Article 161 vests the Governor with the power to suspend, remit, or commute the sentence of any person convicted of an offence against state law.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a Directive Principle of State Policy under Part IV of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Freedom of speech is a Fundamental Right under Article 19, not a Directive Principle.

questions.question: The Madras High Court has original jurisdiction over which union territory?
questions.explanation: The High Court of Madras exercises jurisdiction over the union territory of Puducherry.

questions.question: Which amendment added the word 'Socialist' to the Preamble of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment Act, 1976 inserted 'Socialist' and 'Secular' into the Preamble.

questions.question: Who appoints the Advocate General of a state?
questions.explanation: The Governor of the state appoints the Advocate General under Article 165 of the Constitution.

questions.question: Which Schedule of the Constitution lists the subjects of Panchayats?
questions.explanation: The Eleventh Schedule (added by the 73rd Amendment) contains 29 functional items of Panchayats.

questions.question: As of 2023, which of the following states has a Legislative Council (Vidhan Parishad)?
questions.explanation: Karnataka is one of the few states that still has a bicameral legislature with a Legislative Council; Tamil Nadu abolished its Council in 1986.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution provides for the establishment of Panchayats in every state?
questions.explanation: Article 243 defines key terms, while Article 243B mandates the establishment of Panchayats at village, intermediate, and district levels.

questions.question: The Governor of a state is appointed by the President and holds office during the pleasure of the President. Which Article governs this?
questions.explanation: Article 156 states that the Governor holds office at the pleasure of the President, typically for a term of five years.

questions.question: Under the Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj Act, 1994, what is the minimum age prescribed for a person to be eligible for election as a member of a Panchayat?
questions.explanation: The Act specifies 21 years as the minimum age for contesting Panchayat elections, unlike 18 years for general elections.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a Directive Principle of State Policy under Part IV of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Right to property was a Fundamental Right under Article 31, later repealed by the 44th Amendment; it is now a legal right under Article 300A.

questions.question: In the context of Tamil Nadu, the 'State Election Commission' is responsible for conducting elections to which bodies?
questions.explanation: The State Election Commission conducts elections to both rural (Panchayats) and urban (Municipalities) local bodies in Tamil Nadu.

questions.question: The 'Preamble' of the Indian Constitution declares India to be a 'Sovereign Socialist Secular Democratic Republic'. Which amendment added the words 'Socialist' and 'Secular'?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment Act, 1976, inserted the words 'Socialist' and 'Secular' into the Preamble.

questions.question: Under Article 226 of the Constitution, which court has the power to issue writs for the enforcement of Fundamental Rights?
options: Both Supreme Court and High Court
questions.explanation: Article 226 empowers High Courts to issue writs, while Article 32 empowers the Supreme Court for the same purpose.

questions.question: The 'District Planning Committee' in Tamil Nadu is constituted under which Article of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Article 243ZD mandates the constitution of a District Planning Committee to consolidate plans prepared by Panchayats and Municipalities.

questions.question: Which of the following Fundamental Rights is available only to Indian citizens and not to foreign nationals?
options: Right to life and personal liberty
questions.explanation: Right to freedom of speech (Article 19) is guaranteed only to citizens, while rights like Article 14 and 21 apply to all persons.
